title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 70.
content:
There was an Old Man of Calcutta,
    Who perpetually ate bread and butter;
    Till a great bit of muffin,
    On which he was stuffing,
    Choked that horrid old man of Calcutta.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 70.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
